description: X2-U1 WORLD-grounded fixture: Node.js DEP0033 (events.listenerCount()) deprecated -> revoked. Real, externally-verifiable, impersonal (no person/team -> nothing to redact). Block P (predictable recurrence) asks the original-filing status ('deprecated'); Block U asks the current-ruling status ('revoked'), answerable only by the dep0033-revoked record that C evicted during P and must rematerialize. Out-of-weights is EARNED: the revocation post-dates both engines' weights, proven by the recorded ignorance probe (both answered 'deprecated' cold). recency_weight 0 so the question, not recency, selects the record (same-subject temporal reversal). Gated by harness/check_x2_fixture.py (world-grounded mode).

ignorance_probe.question: In Node.js, the module-level function events.listenerCount() was deprecated long ago under DEP0033. Has that deprecation since been REVOKED (un-deprecated), or does it still stand as deprecated? Answer with exactly one word: revoked or deprecated.
ignorance_probe.interpretation: Neither engine knew DEP0033 was revoked -> the revoked (U) fact is out-of-weights; the dep0033-revoked round-trip record is important (B cannot answer U from weights).

engine_cutoffs_disclosed.claude-opus-4-8: training cutoff ~2026-01; DEP0033 revoked in Node v24.14.0/v25.4.0 (2026) -> post-cutoff, probe-confirmed
engine_cutoffs_disclosed.gpt-oss-20b: local open weights; DEP0033 revocation post-dates weights -> probe-confirmed
